diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic symptom of pleural irritation from a peripheral embolus.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unilateral calf swelling and pain after a long flight

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic presentation of DVT, the source of the pulmonary embolus.
Suggests significant right heart strain reducing cardiac output.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypoxia, right ventricular heave, loud P2

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Pulmonary embolism with right heart strain
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective confirmation; clinician-only findings sealed from telehealth chat.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Pleuritic chest pain, unilateral calf swelling after prolonged immobility, and presyncope together confirm a likely pulmonary embolism from a travel-associated DVT.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency CT pulmonary angiography and immediate anticoagulation (or thrombolysis if massive PE confirmed)
D-dimer and troponin/BNP for risk stratification
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Supports diagnosis and assesses right heart strain severity, though should not delay definitive imaging/treatment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Reassurance and advice to rest at home with simple analgesia
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Would risk fatal progression of an untreated pulmonary embolism.
